items.title: OpenAI Codex: model availability update for ChatGPT-signed-in users
items.why_it_matters: Codex model picker changes can break/alter coding-agent workflows and cost/perf assumptions overnight.
items.brief: Codex changelog notes a model availability/picker update for ChatGPT-auth users (effective Apr 7/8 depending on timezone).
items.details: OpenAI updated the Codex changelog with a note about changing which Codex models appear in the model picker for users who sign in with ChatGPT. If you rely on a specific Codex model name in tooling or docs, verify what’s still selectable and update defaults/pinning accordingly. This kind of change can affect latency, quality, and spend if your workflow silently falls back to a different model. Check your CI/agent configs for hard-coded model IDs and add monitoring for unexpected model switches.

items.title: Claude Code voice mode begins rollout
items.why_it_matters: Voice-driven coding inside an agentic CLI can speed iteration and make “hands-free” debugging/refactoring practical.
items.brief: Boris Cherny says voice mode is rolling out in Claude Code (live for ~5% of users today).
items.details: @bcherny (Claude Code creator) posted that voice mode is rolling out now in Claude Code, starting with ~5% of users and ramping over coming weeks. If your workflow is terminal-first, this is a meaningful UX shift: you can dictate changes, ask for explanations, and drive edits without constant typing. Teams should consider how voice input affects prompt hygiene (accidental secrets) and logging/retention policies. If you’re piloting Claude Code internally, plan a quick evaluation of accuracy and noise handling in your environment.

items.title: Gemini app: release notes page highlights “rolling out starting today” changes
items.why_it_matters: Gemini app capability/limit changes can immediately impact end-user workflows and enterprise support expectations.
items.brief: Google’s Gemini release notes page lists new rollouts “starting today” (check plan limits + model availability).
items.details: Google’s Gemini release notes page includes items labeled as rolling out “starting today,” including model availability and plan-limit changes. For teams standardizing on Gemini for knowledge work, these rollouts can change what users can access (and how often) without a separate product announcement. If you support a mixed fleet (free + paid tiers), capture the new limits and update internal enablement docs. Also verify whether changes are app-only or also affect Gemini API usage.

bullets: OpenAI Codex: model availability/picker update for ChatGPT-auth users (Codex changelog) — https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog — Why: can change your coding-agent defaults/costs.
Claude Code: voice mode rolling out (~5% today) (X @bcherny) — https://x.com/bcherny/status/2028629573722939789 — Why: enables faster, hands-free agentic coding.
Gemini app: new items marked ‘rolling out starting today’ (Gemini release notes) — https://gemini.google/release-notes/ — Why: shifts user access/limits without a big launch post.

detailed.intro: AM digest (America/Chicago) — only items with clear ‘today’ timing from primary/first-party sources or must-track accounts.
